title: St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte
tagline: St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte is a much-loved spiritual town in Karnataka, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: The St. Patrick Church is a Roman Catholic Church situated in the locality of Siddakatte, Bantwal. The church was built in 1926. This church comes under Roman Catholic Diocese of Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Venur
topPlaces.description: Venur or Vēnooru is a small village on the banks of the Phalguni river in Belthangady Taluk, Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka, India. It was once the seat of Jainism and the capital of the Ajila Dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Hosabettu
topPlaces.description: Hosabettu is a village located in Mangalore Taluk of Dakshina Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located near Moodbidri and Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Kuppepadavu
topPlaces.description: Kuppepdavu, is a small town in Karnataka, India, 27 km away from Mangalore city and 340 km away from its State Capital City Bangalore. Bantwal is 15 km away, Moodbidri is 16 km away and Bajpe is 12 km away.

topPlaces.name: Yedapadavu
topPlaces.description: Yedapadavu is a former village (township) in Mangalore taluk, Karnataka, India, that was divided into Badagayedapadavu (North Yedapadavu) and Tenkayedapadavu (South Yedapadavu). Yedapadavu now gives its name, as "Yedapadav" to the gram panchayat for those two villages.

topPlaces.name: Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar
topPlaces.description: The Church of Most Holy Saviour also known as Agrar Church is a historic Roman Catholic Church situated in Bantwal, India. The church was built in 1702, but it is believed that the existence of church was prior to this.

image.alt: Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar

topPlaces.name: Karinjeshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: Sri Karinjeshwara Temple (Kannada : ಕಾರಿಂಜೇಶ್ವರ Kārin̄jēśvara) is Hindu Temple, a famous Lord Shiva temple located Karinja in Bantwala Taluk, Kavalamudur Village, Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. This temple is situated on the peak of Karinja Hill, about 1000 feet above sea level in Kodyamale hills, Karinjeshwara.

topPlaces.name: Tenkayedapadavu
topPlaces.description: Tenkayedapadavu is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, it is under Mangalore taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

items.note: Venur or Vēnooru is a small village on the banks of the Phalguni river in Belthangady Taluk, Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka, India. It was once the seat of Jainism and the capital of the Ajila Dynasty.
Hosabettu is a village located in Mangalore Taluk of Dakshina Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located near Moodbidri and Mangalore.

Kuppepdavu, is a small town in Karnataka, India, 27 km away from Mangalore city and 340 km away from its State Capital City Bangalore. Bantwal is 15 km away, Moodbidri is 16 km away and Bajpe is 12 km away.

Yedapadavu is a former village (township) in Mangalore taluk, Karnataka, India, that was divided into Badagayedapadavu (North Yedapadavu) and Tenkayedapadavu (South Yedapadavu). Yedapadavu now gives its name, as "Yedapadav" to the gram panchayat for those two villages.

items.activity: Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar
items.note: The Church of Most Holy Saviour also known as Agrar Church is a historic Roman Catholic Church situated in Bantwal, India. The church was built in 1702, but it is believed that the existence of church was prior to this.

Sri Karinjeshwara Temple (Kannada : ಕಾರಿಂಜೇಶ್ವರ Kārin̄jēśvara) is Hindu Temple, a famous Lord Shiva temple located Karinja in Bantwala Taluk, Kavalamudur Village, Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. This temple is situated on the peak of Karinja Hill, about 1000 feet above sea level in Kodyamale hills, Karinjeshwara.
Tenkayedapadavu is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, it is under Mangalore taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (27 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte?
faq.a: Stays in St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte?
faq.a: The top attractions are Venur, Hosabettu, Kuppepadavu, Yedapadavu, Church of Most Holy Saviour, Agrar.
